title: pellaea columbina
intro: Pellaea columbina, also known as the False Bracken or False Lady Fern, is a type of fern native to South Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Pellaea columbina include its ability to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Pellaea columbina is often used in infusions, decoctions, and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
